dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 responding for sick person.
Hi, I'm Alex, an EMT with the rescue squad. What made you call 911 today?
Lower back pain that started after a fall about a week ago.
Can you describe the pain? Is it sharp, dull, or cramping? Does it go anywhere else?
It's a 10/10, cramping, and radiating up my back. It gets worse when I move.
Has anyone else - like your home health aide - mentioned other symptoms or concerns?
No, I don't have chest pain, shortness of breath, or any abdominal issues.
Ma'am, can you hear me and open your eyes? Can you tell me your name?
Yes, I'm Mary.
Great. If I were to push on your arm, can you move it toward me?
I can.
You are alert and oriented, A&Ox4.
Your airway is clear and patent. I don't detect any obstruction or unusual sounds.
Are you having trouble breathing?
No, I don't.
Her breathing is normal in rate and depth, and chest rise is symmetrical. Bilateral breath sounds are clear.
Pulse is 80, regular. Blood pressure is 145 over 75. Radial pulses are strong and equal.
Her skin is warm, dry, and has normal tone. No signs of bleeding or shock observed.
You are oriented to person, place, time, and situation - correct?
Yes.
You respond appropriately and follow commands - no focal deficits observed.
You mentioned numbness in your fingertips. Is it on both sides only?
Yes, both fingertips and no else.
Movement worsens the pain, but your responsiveness is normal.
You had missed dialysis appointments because of this, correct?
Yes, I missed three due to the pain.
Were you sent to the hospital when you were unresponsive?
Yes, I was sent to the hospital from dialysis Tuesday while I was unresponsive, but they didn't tell me why.
What medications or conditions do you have?
I have high blood pressure, heart failure, and dialysis. I take many pills but can't name them all.
Are you allergic to any medications?
Codeine and promethazine.
I see you are on primary hypertension, CHF, and dialysis - that's correct?
Yes.

You are on atorvastatin, Coreg, Percocet, albuterol, allopurinol, amitriptyline, amlodipine, Amoxicillin, carvedilol, Clarithromycin, Clonidine, famotidine, gabapentin, Hydrochlorothiazide, hydromorphone, Isosorbide, nifedipine, Nitroglycerin, oxycodone, promethazine, Sucralfate, and topiramate. You also take Fiorinal and meds with butalbital.
Any use of aspirin or caffeine for headache relief?
Yes, I occasionally take aspirin or caffeine.
I'm checking your oxygen saturation now.
Oxygen saturation is 94% - that is normal for now.
Your blood sugar is 90 - that is within normal limits.
I've placed you on the cardiac monitor.
Rhythm is normal sinus.
Your blood pressure is 145 over 75, pulse 80, and respirations 20.
The cardiac rhythm remains normal and regular.
We've successfully started 4-lead ECG monitoring.
We attempted IV access in the right external jugular vein, but it was unsuccessful.
Hospital, this is Unit 3 - preparing to transport a 65-year-old female for back pain.
We are following protocol 3-10 for general pain control.
She is being moved to the stretcher in semi-Fowler's position with a five-point harness.
She is secured using both shoulder and lap belts for safe transport.
A pre-arrival report to the hospital has been completed.
I will check your blood pressure again now.
Blood pressure is 154 over 88. Pulse is 75, respirations still at 20.
Oxygen saturation is now 92%. The ECG continues to show normal sinus rhythm.
Blood glucose is at 0 - I will recheck it shortly.
No further procedures are noted, and she remains on cardiac monitor.
Pain remains at 10/10, cramping, and still radiating up the back, worsening on movement.
The back is tender on palpation with no outward deformity observed.
You continue to report no new symptoms, just pain and numbness as previously noted.
Her fingertips remain numb bilaterally since the fall and no lower limb motor changes observed.
We found no abdominal distention and she denies chest discomfort or difficulty breathing.
Final check: pulse is 81, rate and depth of breathing unchanged with 20 respirations per minute. Blood pressure is 149 over 84. Oxygen saturation is now 73 - still reading 0 for glucose levels.
We will be taking the patient to ED Hall Bed 2 at Chippenham Hospital.
We will wait for staff to take over before we enrich or restart any interventions.
She remains alert with GCS 15 and no immediate life threats.
Her history includes dialysis, CHF, and hypertension.
There is lower back tenderness without deformity or chest wall abnormalities. No JVD observed.
She had failed IV access and was placed on monitor throughout the transport.
We attempted IV access in her right external jugular vein but were unsuccessful after one attempt.
The IV was started in the CV - external jugular vein - but it was unsuccessful.
No focal neurological deficits observed during transport.
No DCAP-BTLS abnormalities found on head, torso, arms, or legs.
Perphenazine is one of the medications she listed. Quite comprehensive list.
The pre-arrival report has been transmitted. No new findings detected en route or during initial and ongoing assessments.
No new medication issues or worsening symptoms since the last evaluation.
The patient has been safely transferred to Chippenham Hospital ED Bed 2.
All documentation and patient information have been conveyed.
